name: Fendu Boulangerie
tagline: Kaimuki's French-inspired bakery — croissants, kouign-amann, and naturally leavened breads that rival mainland artisan bakeries.
talkStory: Opened by pastry chef Pono Bumatay, 'fendu' refers to a traditional French bread-shaping technique. Bumatay trained at top pastry programs before returning to his hometown of Honolulu to open on Waialae Ave. The naturally leavened breads use a sourdough starter he's maintained for years, and the kouign-amann rivals b. Patisserie's at a lower price point. Popular items sell out by 10am, so serious pastry people arrive at opening.
